body_txt: Sure, an Eye-Obscuring Hat is a cool look to make one look more mysterious, but there's a small catch: if your eyes are obscured, how do you see? While it's easy enough to just pop the brim up every so often, if your hat's a bit too large, that's not as convenient. However, there's always another way. Cue the Hat Mask. Just cut a couple of holes in your hat where it falls over your face, and you've got your vision back. Depending on the wearer, it can increase "mystery", but in the end, it also just might look ridiculous. Most of the time, these are top hats or fedoras that use the hatband like a Domino Mask , but floppy stocking hats are also a common sight. Compare with Living Hat , a step above "eyes on a hat" in terms of headgear. Not to be confused with Coat, Hat, Mask , though the latter two costume features can be merged into one for the sake of this trope.
